title: Wrong Patient Context Retained After Chart Switch
scenario: A clinician begins a medication workflow for one patient, switches charts, and the execution interface retains the prior patient context.
initial_state: The medication workflow was opened under a valid patient identity context.
state_transition: The active chart context changed before execution while the medication execution context did not update.
execution_boundary: Medication administration is evaluated immediately before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Patient identity continuity must remain preserved between authorization, evaluation, and execution.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the patient identity binding at the execution boundary no longer matches the current clinical context.
proof_conclusion: A clinical action must fail closed when patient identity continuity cannot be established at execution time.
clinical_action: Administer medication from retained chart context
